echo (Energy and Commodity Holistic Optimiser)
  • »
  • echo API »
  • echo.utils module
  • View page source

echo.utils module

echo.utils.add_time_and_expansion_index_to_values(values, time_periods, expansion_periods)

Takes some input (values) - could be array, or int. Adds a time_period and expansion period key.

echo.utils.create_input_output_pts_from_coefficients(temp_coef, input_coef, temperature_array, xpts, time_periods)

Generates a set of output (y) points based on two coefficient arrays, one that applies to the input variable and one that applies to temperature, and non decreasing xpts. :param temp_coef: list of coefficients, right to left in increasing order :param input_coef: list of coefficients, right to left in increasing order :param temperature_array: list of temperature data for time intervals T :param xpts: number of x points we want to do our piecewise evaluation over :param time_periods: number of optimisation time periods

Returns

a dict of lists, where keys are the index set, defining the set of domain breakpoints for the piecewise linear function. y: a dict of lists, where keys are the index set, defining the set of domain breakpoints for the piecewise linear function.

Return type

x

echo.utils.create_named_constraint_with_rule(model, con_name, rule)

Util function for creating pyomo constraints from a rule.

echo.utils.do_multivariate_regression(X, y)

Performs multivariate regression on provided data. :param X: :param Y:

Returns

coefficient array R^2

echo.utils.fix_port_variable(model, var_name: str, new_values: echo.echo_validators.ArrayType, expansion_periods=1)

Updates existing pyomo variable to have fixed values. :param model: pyomo concrete model :param var_name: pyomo variable name (str) :param new_values: array or list of new values :param expansion_periods: number of expansion periods (int)

echo.utils.generate_array_constraint(constraint, time_periods: int, expansion_periods: int) → dict
Parameters

constraint – float or array

Returns

a formatted dict to be used in constraining a variable

Return type

d

echo.utils.generate_input_output_values_from_polynomial_coeff_array(coeff_array, pts)

Generates x and y values for the polynomial function with coefficients defined by the coeff array :param coeff array: list of polynomial function coefficients :param pts: list of x values for which we want to evaluate the function described by the coeff array

echo.utils.generate_piecewise_input_output_arrays(coeff_array, pts)

Generates x and y values for the polynomial function with coefficients defined by the coeff array :param coeff array: list of polynomial function coefficients, indexed by time :param pts: list of x values for which we want to evaluate the function described by the coeff array

echo.utils.set_float_var_bounds(model, var_name: str, ub: float, lb: float) → None

Updates the bounds on a pyomo variable. Only floats can be used as bounds. :param model: pyomo concrete model :param var_name: variable name (str) corresponding to a variable in the model :param ub: upper bound value, or None :param lb: lower bound value, or None

Returns

None

echo.utils.set_var_bounds_from_dict(var, ub: dict, lb: dict) → None

Updates the bounds on a pyomo variable using an array of floats. :param var: pyomo variable :param ub: dict of floats, where dict keys match variable index sets, or None :param lb: dict of floats, where dict keys match variable index sets, or None

Returns

None
